target.title: docs(backup): describe manual restore flow
target.body:
## Summary

- Document that `openclaw backup` currently supports create/verify, not a first-class restore command.
- Add manual restore guidance: extract archive, inspect `manifest.json`, stop OpenClaw, restore only needed payload paths, verify archive first, and run doctor after restart.

Fixes #77849

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: the backup docs listed create and verify flows but did not tell operators how to restore from a backup archive, and the CLI does not currently expose `openclaw backup restore`.

Real environment tested:
- Repository checkout: `/root/projects/openclaw-easy-docs`
- PR head: `98e0243397`
- Base: `origin/main`
- File changed: `docs/cli/backup.md`

Commands run:
- `rg -n "openclaw backup (create|verify|restore)|restore|payload" docs/cli/backup.md src -g '*.{ts,tsx,md,mdx}'`
- `git diff origin/main...HEAD -- docs/cli/backup.md`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/check-docs-mdx.mjs docs/cli/backup.md`

Evidence after the fix:
- The docs now include a `Restore from an archive` section that says there is no `openclaw backup restore` command today.
- The new section tells operators to extract the archive, inspect `manifest.json`, stop OpenClaw, restore only needed payload paths, run `openclaw backup verify <archive>`, restart, and run `openclaw doctor`.
- The existing docs and source references confirmed the CLI examples are create/verify only and the archive layout uses manifest-declared payload paths.
- `git diff --check` completed with no whitespace errors.
- GitHub CI `check-docs` passed on this PR.

What was not tested:
- Local MDX parsing could not run in this checkout because `@mdx-js/mdx` is not installed in `node_modules`; GitHub CI ran `check-docs` successfully.
- No live restore was performed because this is documentation for the current manual restore procedure, not a new restore implementation.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- GitHub CI `check-docs`
target.author: clawsean
